card_description: John Allegro is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick refines his view of theolepsy as an "Internal Theophany" where the immanent God in the "Atman" is proved identical to the Brahman without."

linked_segments.summary: Dick refines his view of theolepsy as an "Internal Theophany" where the immanent God in the "Atman" is proved identical to the Brahman without. He defines the Kingdom of God as our ancestral "Garden"
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_12.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick interprets the Eucharist as an authentic transportation to an "orthogonal universe" where Christ still lives. He links his experience to the "Feast of Agape" and the legalistic/Roman suppression
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_05.txt
